candidates.stmt: Rachel is a banker and a committed community advocate. During the pandemic, she worked as a community care assistant across Barnehurst, Slade Green, Bexleyheath and Bexley, supporting elderly residents and gaining a strong understanding of local needs. As a councillor, she will work with Daniel Francis MP to improve safety, roads and youth provision.

Matthew is a long-standing community organiser with over 30 years of experience in local campaigning and public service. He has supported schools, neighbourhood forums and local safety initiatives across London. He will focus on strengthening community networks in Barnehurst, improving engagement, and supporting practical solutions for families and local services.

It has been a pleasure and an honour to serve as a councillor since 2018.  I live in the ward  with my family and I am a trustee and treasurer for St Augustine’s Pre School. I am embedded in Belvedere and will continue to fight to improve the area for the benefit of all those in this community.

Jeremy is an Employment Paralegal and has served as Belvedere councillor since October 2024. He champions better housing in Bexley and tackles anti-social behaviour, already securing two new CCTV cameras for the community.

I’ve lived in Bexley since 1995.  I worked for over 30 years as an Educational Psychologist on behalf of vulnerable children, young people, and their families.  I believe that residents are poorly served by an indifferent Conservative Council and that more Labour councillors are needed to better represent the needs of local people and shape and influence decisions for the benefit of all.

Sarah Barry has lived in the Erith and Thamesmead constituency and taught in local secondary schools for 25 years. She is an active member of the Greenwich and Bexley Green Party and is involved in many aspects of community life, including service as a volunteer for local Cub, Scout and Guides groups, as well as conservation groups and mental health charities. Sarah is standing for the Green Party because she is committed to contributing towards a fairer and more sustainable future. Sarah is standing in Erith and Thamesmead constituency.

Christine is a local community champion who has previously served as an elected Councillor for eight years and stood for Belvedere Ward at the last local elections. She is passionate about improving services for the vulnerable, supporting local charities and working to keep our streets safe. 

Christine is campaigning hard to oppose the Labour Government's nasty and mean cut to the Winter Fuel Payment, which could affect around 2,000 residents in Belvedere. She's supporting the Council's efforts to secure improvements to public transport including the extension of the DLR and Elizabeth Line and Thameslink services stopping at Belvedere, and the Council's investments in improvements to the environment, street cleaning and strong action on flytipping. 

Christine knows how important it is for people to feel safe and will work with the police to re-establish a local policing panel so residents can have their voices heard and set priorities to combat crime and anti-social behaviour. 

For too long Belvedere has been let down by Labour Councillors who have taken the area for granted and a Labour MP who backs the early release of criminals, cuts to winter fuel payments and higher taxes. 

At this by election, the people of Belvedere can elect a strong local champion who will stand up for them, put their interests first and work to make Belvedere Even Better. Vote Christine Bishop on 17 October.

𝖨 𝖺𝗆 𝗌𝗍𝖺𝗇𝖽𝗂𝗇𝗀 𝗍𝗈 𝖻𝖾 𝗒𝗈𝗎𝗋 𝖼𝗈𝗎𝗇𝖼𝗂𝗅𝗅𝗈𝗋 𝖻𝖾𝖼𝖺𝗎𝗌𝖾 𝖡𝖾𝗅𝗏𝖾𝖽𝖾𝗋𝖾 𝖽𝖾𝗌𝖾𝗋𝗏𝖾𝗌 𝖺 𝗋𝖾𝗉𝗋𝖾𝗌𝖾𝗇𝗍𝖺𝗍𝗂𝗏𝖾 𝗐𝗁𝗈 𝗅𝗂𝗌𝗍𝖾𝗇𝗌, 𝗎𝗇𝖽𝖾𝗋𝗌𝗍𝖺𝗇𝖽𝗌 𝖺𝗇𝖽 𝗐𝗈𝗋𝗄𝗌 𝗁𝖺𝗋𝖽 𝖿𝗈𝗋 𝖾𝗏𝖾𝗋𝗒 𝗋𝖾𝗌𝗂𝖽𝖾𝗇𝗍. 𝖬𝗒 𝖼𝗈𝗆𝗆𝗂𝗍𝗆𝖾𝗇𝗍 𝗂𝗌 𝗍𝗈 𝗁𝗈𝗇𝖾𝗌𝗍𝗒, 𝗂𝗇𝗍𝖾𝗀𝗋𝗂𝗍𝗒 𝖺𝗇𝖽 𝗉𝗋𝖺𝖼𝗍𝗂𝖼𝖺𝗅 𝖺𝖼𝗍𝗂𝗈𝗇 𝗍𝗁𝖺𝗍 𝗂𝗆𝗉𝗋𝗈𝗏𝖾𝗌 𝖽𝖺𝗂𝗅𝗒 𝗅𝗂𝖿𝖾. 𝖨 𝗐𝗂𝗅𝗅 𝖿𝗈𝖼𝗎𝗌 𝗈𝗇 𝗋𝖾𝖽𝗎𝖼𝗂𝗇𝗀 𝗍𝗁𝖾 𝗉𝗋𝖾𝗌𝗌𝗎𝗋𝖾𝗌 𝗈𝖿 𝗍𝗁𝖾 𝗋𝗂𝗌𝗂𝗇𝗀 𝖼𝗈𝗌𝗍 𝗈𝖿 𝗅𝗂𝗏𝗂𝗇𝗀, 𝗌𝗍𝗋𝖾𝗇𝗀𝗍𝗁𝖾𝗇𝗂𝗇𝗀 𝗅𝗈𝖼𝖺𝗅 𝗌𝖾𝗋𝗏𝗂𝖼𝖾𝗌, 𝗌𝗎𝗉𝗉𝗈𝗋𝗍𝗂𝗇𝗀 𝖿𝖺𝗆𝗂𝗅𝗂𝖾𝗌 𝖺𝗇𝖽 𝖻𝗎𝗌𝗂𝗇𝖾𝗌𝗌𝖾𝗌, 𝖺𝗇𝖽 𝖾𝗇𝗌𝗎𝗋𝗂𝗇𝗀 𝗈𝗎𝗋 𝗇𝖾𝗂𝗀𝗁𝖻𝗈𝗎𝗋𝗁𝗈𝗈𝖽𝗌 𝖺𝗋𝖾 𝗌𝖺𝖿𝖾, 𝖼𝗅𝖾𝖺𝗇 𝖺𝗇𝖽 𝗐𝖾𝗅𝖼𝗈𝗆𝗂𝗇𝗀. 𝖶𝗂𝗍𝗁 𝗒𝗈𝗎𝗋 𝗌𝗎𝗉𝗉𝗈𝗋𝗍, 𝖨 𝗐𝗂𝗅𝗅 𝖻𝖾 𝖺 𝗌𝗍𝗋𝗈𝗇𝗀 𝖺𝗇𝖽 𝖽𝖾𝖽𝗂𝖼𝖺𝗍𝖾𝖽 𝗏𝗈𝗂𝖼𝖾 𝖿𝗈𝗋 𝗈𝗎𝗋 𝖼𝗈𝗆𝗆𝗎𝗇𝗂𝗍𝗒.

My Statement to the Voters of Bexleyheath

•	Reform UK are the only party to replace a complacent Conservative council in Bexley and to fight for better local services, controlled development, safer streets, and value for your money.

You are paying more, but getting less
•	Council tax has risen, but services are not improving 
•	Waste collection, roads, and local amenities are slipping 
•	Reform are the “value-for-money watchdogs” and our track record in local councils across the country is proof we can deliver on local issues that matter

We believe in controlled growth, not over-development
•	We care about our beautiful Bexley and its rich history
•	We will oppose any over-development without proper infrastructure 
•	We will protect our precious green spaces and our local character
•	We will ensure planning decisions and transport needs are properly considered for the good of the people of Bexley
•	We will support local business, encourage economic growth in Bexleyheath town centre, and ensure shoppers have a better experience - especially on transport and parking issues

**You CAN make a difference!**

•	Labour and the Conservatives have broken Britain with broken promises and a constant disregard for the views and genuine concerns of the majority of the British people.  Reform UK is the only credible Party with vision, purpose, and the political will to put the Great back into Britain.

•	Reform UK is the only party to fix the Labour and previous Conservative government’s appalling legacy of debt, increased crime, mass migration, and weakened security of the UK.  We need to send a message that this is not good enough. It's time for a change.

•	A **vote for Reform UK in the local election on May 7** is the first step towards a better Bexley and a better Britain.

**We WILL listen to you. We WILL act on your behalf.  Your vote WILL count**

Elections offer choice, but on Thursday 7th May 2026, Bexley has an opportunity to
change the direction of our borough for a better future with investment and stop the
managed decline of public services in Bexley.
This year’s election is the opportunity to look to the future and build better housing for everyone, including good quality homes to buy, more family homes, genuinely affordable housing and council housing for the first time in a generation, so future generations can afford to live in Bexley and invest in a better future with new fresh ideas, not out of date and tired ideas that have not worked for years and cannot work in the future.  
Bexley Labour has a plan to deal with the challenges this borough faces. We will work constructively with the Labour Mayor of London, Sadiq Khan, and the Labour Government to deliver better public services and more housing for everyone.

Having lived in the Borough all his life, Frazer is proud to say he’s a local lad through and through. He currently work as a Senior Caseworker for an MP in Buckinghamshire and it is through this role that he not only understands the problems many residents face, but he also has a proven track record in knowing how to go about fixing them. Frazer want to bring these skills and experiences home to help residents in Blackfen and Lamorbey, and across Bexley.

Frazer is also engaged in charity work and community events – locally he recently volunteered at Bexfest and the Danson Fireworks which raised thousands of pounds for local charities and Bexley’s looked after children.

David has lived in Bexley since 1984, growing up in the Borough, attending local primary and secondary schools, and previously working in a local small business. He has represented communities in Bexley as a local Councillor for 12 of the last 16 years and has also served terms as a school governor and a trustee of a local charity.

David has a strong record helping residents with issues and campaigning for road safety improvements, action to prevent crime and anti-social behaviour, and support for local families and businesses affected by the Covid pandemic. As the Council’s Cabinet Member for Resources, David has developed schemes to support low income households, worked with colleagues to get funding distributed to businesses, and is proud of overseeing the financing of Council’s housing company BexleyCo as it built it’s first housing development for local people to move into.

Over the last four years, David has also worked cross-Party and pan-London to develop and oversee a multi-million pound programme to commission charities to combat homelessness and tackle domestic and sexual abuse.

A working mother of two children in local schools, Sevda will champion a Crayford family hub. She will ensure families access early support and equal educational opportunities across the community.

A Crayford resident for over 20 years, Colin is committed to making Crayford a better place to live. He will champion residents’ voices and fight for fair housing opportunities for future generations.

A proud South Londoner, Nathan champions improved public transport in Crayford. He will deliver better connections, reliable services, and accessible travel, supporting families, businesses, and a cleaner, safer community.

Dear Voter, I have been a member of the Liberal Democrats for a few years now. The reason why I chose to join them is that they are the only party which, in my opinion, offers a balanced political ideology. In government it is the only Party that can unite the country and, at local level, it has the interest of all residents. 
The reason that now I decided to become a candidate is simple, I hope to make a difference. I always moan about things in Bexley not working as they should be but, I have not been able to do anything about it. I want to voice my concerns, and be heard, or to offer a solution to the various problems. So, instead of being a Facebook worrier (other platforms are available-and I do use them 😉) I hope you give me the opportunity to make a difference and to be your voice also
Thank you

East Wickham resident since 2004. First elected as its Councillor (Conservative) in the 2009 by-election. Current term of office 2022-26.  Former police officer and current IT Consultant. Married with teenage son. Dog owner. Grew up on a Charlton Council Estate. I am a parishioner of St. Mary's church, a member of the East Wickham Safer Neighbourhood Panel, and a Discover Welling vounteer.

Over the last four years, I have dealt with over 550 items of casework and reported hundreds of FixMyStreet.com issues.  I've also raised hundreds of issues with local housing providers, the police, utility companies, the MP, and our London Assembly Member.

Promoted by Andrew Kennedy on behalf of Steven Hall at 17 Church Road, Bexleyheath, Kent, DA7 4DD.

Caroline grew up in East Wickham ward and lived there for large part of her life. She still lives in Welling and works in the City of London.

Caroline has been a Councillor for 12 years, for the former St Michael’s ward and, for the past four years, for East Wickham ward.

Caroline’s Council interests include children’s services and education, adults’ services and community, as well as ensuring that Bexley residents have value for money in all services the Council provides.

During the 2018-22 term, Caroline has chaired the Children's and Adults' Services Overview and Scrutiny Committee, the Pensions Committee and the Communities Overview and Scrutiny Committee.

I have lived locally for 17 years with my wife and two daughters. I am a Chemical Engineer with a local business that employs local people.

I am a parishioner of St. Michael's Church (East Wickham) where I was baptised. I regularly walk my two dogs on the East Wickham Open Space. Furthermore, I'm a Discover Welling volunteer and a member of the East Wickham Safer Neighbourhood Panel helping to set police priorities in East Wickham.

Over the past year, I've joined the East Wickham Councillor Action Team and have personally spoken to over 2500 residents to establish their views and any concerns. We've cleared litter from Network Rail property in Upper Wickham Lane, Central Avenue, William Foster Lane, and the pedestrian railway bridge in Montrose Avenue.

Promoted by Andrew Kennedy on behalf of David Li at 17 Church Road, Bexleyheath, Kent, DA7 4DD

My name is Baris Lefkonuklu and I’m standing for Reform UK in East Wickham.
I believe every family here deserves to feel safe, supported and proud of our community. Too many residents tell me they feel ignored — that must change.

If elected, I will focus on the issues that matter most to local people:

• Tackling crime and anti‑social behaviour
• Cleaning up our streets and protecting our green spaces
• Challenging waste and demanding accountability
• Making sure your voice is heard at the council

Together we can build a cleaner, safer and more confident East Wickham.
Bexley is getting left behind under the Conservatives, bexley racked up depts of over £250 million. That’s 23k A DAY in interest alone. Bexley is in desperate need of change. Let’s send the uni party packing on May 7th. Vote Reform.

I am a Bexley resident for over 25 years.  Like many residents I am disappointed that services in Bexley are  declining.  As your local councillor I will prioritise neighbourhood policing, youth engagement, tackle antisocial behaviour and of course fixing potholes and improving road safety, and keeping our streets clean.

As a community leader, I have seen first hand the frustration that many residents face, and I am committed to addressing these.

Professionally I have a PhD in Computer Sciences and Informatics, I work in the IT sector.

I am an East Wickham Ward resident and  have been a resident of the London Borough of Bexley for over twenty years and I have witnessed the decline in our roads and safety  in our communities under the Conservative administration.

As a Broadcast Journalist and community leader I am committed to addressing these key issues. with over 30 years' experience in the broadcast media industry, graduating from our local University in Greenwich.

I am resident of Bexley for over forty years and I have witnessed the decline in access to housing and basic services and the growing disrepair of our communities under the Conservative administration.

I have considerable experience having worked  in the public sector and I am committed to addressing these key issues.

Nicola has represented Erith since 2018, proudly adding a strong voice with a passion for seeking the best for our town. She has fought hard for decent homes, a cleaner town, and the revitalisation of Erith. She organised Bexley’s first Pride event and supports local community groups and businesses. FB @cllrnicolataylor

Chris has lived in Erith for over 25 years and is an experienced councillor, having previously led the Council and served as Mayor. Chris plays an active part in Erith life, strongly representing local residents. He is a headteacher by profession.

I have lived in the London Borough of Bexley for over 20 years, and it is not only my home but also the home of my children and grandchildren. Over that time, many changes have taken place, and some of them have left me concerned about the future for myself and my family.

It is time for a positive change—one that restores hope over fear and ensures everyone in our borough feels welcomed and included.

More broadly, I support the Green Party’s position on public ownership of essential services such as utilities, railways and the Post Office. I also believe the Green Party is the only political party that truly recognises the climate emergency and the devastating consequences of continuing to ignore it for our precious planet.

Ensuring future generations can afford to live in Bexley and invest in a better future for themselves and our borough.
Bexley Labour has a plan to deal with the challenges this borough faces. We will work constructively with the Labour Mayor of London, Sadiq Khan, and the Labour Government to deliver better public services and more housing for everyone.

I have been an active volunteer in Falconwood and Welling for 22 years. I worked in education for 30 years and am a director of Wasteless Market: a non-profit company with a focus on sustainability. 
I am standing for the Green Party because I believe in social justice and care about the natural environment. I believe it’s important for councillors to work together respectfully whatever their political party. I have good working relationships with other local councillors and I aim to continue those if elected.

I am a proud Longlands resident since the 70s, and am grateful for the brilliant care my parents receive from local GPs, carers and social services in Bexley. I want to see better value for money for taxpayers, cleaner streets, safer roads and protected green spaces so our kids and vulnerable can breathe clean air. I want to see our young people thrive and I welcome government support for Bexley family and youth hubs. Bexley needs more homes so families stay close by and young people are not forced to move further out, away from better opportunities and fewer transport links. With a Labour government in power, these ambitions are in reaching distance and I will work hard as a councillor to bring these opportunities to Bexley.

Professionally, I am a Chartered Accountant with strategic finance and governance experience, lately in the public sector. (Local Government, Rail, NHS).

I chose to make Longlands my (blended) family home after experiencing the joys of many other local South East London boroughs since moving to London in 1995.  As such I have genuine comparisons when I say we live in such a fantastic area.

While I have 20+ years of experience working in local schools, including governing, that’s not all I am.  I’m also one of the Run Directors at a local parkrun and have run both of the parkruns in Bexley.  My experiences include family court, SEND and general children’s and young people’s services.  I want to serve our community and you.

Christine Landman is an experienced health professional, adores her grandchildren and champions sustainability and clean waterways. Having lived locally since the 1970s, she wants a fairer future for young people who feel left behind. She backs Bexley Labour’s drive for genuinely affordable homes to help keep families rooted in our community.

I’m a Longlands resident and will often be seen out running or cycling in our area. I value a safe, clean, and well cared for community. Local governance is about that before anything else, regardless of your party affiliation.

I am a pragmatic leader with decades of experience in defence and technology. I’ve worked with governments, corporations, and community groups. I’m used to finding solutions that work for multiple interests and I’m not afraid to admit to and address difficult problems.

I am a British Sikh resident living in the local area for over 42 years.  I worked for over 33 years at the Hovis Bakery. Throughout this time, I have seen Northumberland Heath evolve and become the thriving community it is today. I am multilingual speaking fluent English, Punjabi, Hindi and Urdu. I have spent time speaking with local communities and I understand their needs. If I am elected as a councillor, I will work with a people-first mentality, ensuring our communities voices are heard.

Temilola has been a Bexley resident for eight years who has built strong community roots both personally and professionally. She is a primary school teacher passionate about early development, helping children build confidence, resilience and a love of learning. She also runs roller skating festivals and mentoring workshops for young people.

I am standing as a Conservative candidate for Northumberland Heath to ensure our community is properly heard and represented.

As a British Army veteran, I bring discipline, responsibility, and a strong commitment to service. I will be present in the community, listen to residents, and follow through on local issues.

I am particularly focused on improving engagement around planning decisions and the local environment, and ensuring residents’ voices are properly represented within the council.

My name is Daniel Kersten, and I am honoured to be your Reform UK parliamentary candidate for Weald of Kent in the upcoming General Election. Though new to politics, I am passionate about advocating for change that resonates deeply with my own values and views.  

We must defend core democratic values, civil liberties, and the right to free speech. Britain has an amazing culture, unbreakable communities, and an incredible heritage. It's time to stop all the nonsense holding us back and move forward together. 

I am a father of two beautiful children and have been a foster carer for the past 12 years. Starting work at the age of 14, I have built a career spanning over 30 years in the private sector, working with British startups and global corporations. My personal experiences have fuelled my dedication to advocating for policies that empower individuals and communities, and I bring the same principles of successful business management—delivering results, creating value, and working with dedication and integrity—to my public service.

As your Member of Parliament, I will fight for our community and work tirelessly to address the challenges impacting our health, education, and overall well-being.

I will represent the majority and bring back common sense, my priories are:

 Secure our borders and fix immigration, reducing pressure on housing and critical services. Reform the health service by cutting bureaucracy and top-heavy management structures, freeing up funds for more frontline staff. 

Ensure lower taxes and higher salaries while drastically reducing the size of government.

We must help people off benefits to get qualifications and back to work.

I will fight to protect freedom of speech and personal liberties and work to stop wasteful spending in Net Zero, Ulez and LTNs.

 In our constituency, my priorities are:

 Secure proper Infrastructure

Elections offer choice, but on Thursday 7th May 2026, Bexley has an opportunity to
change the direction of our borough for a better future with investment and stop the
managed decline of public services in Bexley.
This year’s election is the opportunity to look to the future and build better housing for everyone, including good quality homes to buy, more family homes, genuinely affordable housing and council housing for the first time in a generation, so future generations can afford to live in Bexley and invest in a better future with new fresh ideas, not out of date and tired ideas that have not worked for years and cannot work in the future.  
Bexley Labour has a plan to deal with the challenges this borough faces. We will work constructively with the Labour Mayor of London, Sadiq Khan, and the Labour Government to deliver better public services and more housing for everyone.

Stefano has served as a councillor since 2010 and has led the Bexley Labour Group since 2021. He will continue to fight for better transport links, genuinely affordable housing and cleaner streets, while working with residents in Slade Green to deliver improved community facilities and public spaces through £20m Pride in Place funding.

Donna served as a councillor from 1986 to 2006, including as Leader and Deputy Leader of the Council. She brings extensive experience and will support efforts to improve transport, deliver affordable housing, clean up streets and invest in stronger community facilities across Slade Green.

### **To the People of St Mary’s & St James **

I’ve called Bexley home for over 15 years. I chose to raise my three daughters here because of our strong community, green spaces, and the sense of safety we used to have. But like you, I’ve watched standards slip year after year.

**Bexley Council has had it too easy for too long. Reform UK will change that.**  
I’m standing because our borough deserves better services, sensible development, safer streets, and a council that respects your money.

#### **I see the decline every day — and I know you do too**

As a professional driver, I’m out on our roads daily. I see the potholes that damage cars and endanger cyclists. I see the fly-tipping blighting places like North cray Road , Vicarage Road sitting there for weeks. I see how abandoned areas attract antisocial behaviour.

And as a parent, I worry. I want my daughters — and all our children — to grow up in a Bexley where they feel safe walking home, where parks aren’t vandalised, and where the council actually deals with problems.

**Council tax up. Standards down. Enough.**

Your bills have gone up, but has Bexley got better?  
- Rubbish left uncollected
- Roads crumbling  
- Local facilities neglected

**Reform UK are the taxpayers’ watchdog.** Where we’ve won seats across the country, we’ve focused on one thing: fixing the basics and giving residents value for money. I’ll bring that same focus here.

#### **Protect Bexley, don’t concrete over it**

I love this borough — its history, its green spaces, its community feel. That’s why I’ll fight:
- **No** to over-development without the roads, schools, and GP surgeries to support it
- **Yes** to protecting our parks and open spaces
- **Yes** to planning that puts Bexley residents before developers  
- **Yes** to tackling fly-tipping and antisocial behaviour with proper enforcement and CCTV in hotspots
- **Yes** to backing Bexley village— sorting parking, improving transport, and helping local shops thrive

#### **It’s

Voters, use your vote to send a clear message, on both local and national issues, we need better social care and more affordable housing, we need opening rather shutting police stations and a greater police presence.

I've grown up and live in Thamesmead.. I have worked in a variety of human rights organisations, charities as well as in Parliament, advocating for the very communities I am a part of.   We are all frustrated by the lack of care and consideration we have been shown by  Conservative-led Bexley Council.  If elected, I will strive to be a visible, vocal and passionate voice for you and and will champion young people, tackle inequality, address housing shortages and bring communities together.

I live in Thamesmead East and would like to be given the chance to help make the ward an even better place to live. It has a thriving community with housing and retail developments currently being built, alongside beautiful greenery and a picturesque walk along the river Thames.  Chronic fly tipping and antisocial behaviour in the form of noisy moped riders are everyday problems I will prioritise.

Abi is a local parent committed to supporting families. She will improve access to children’s services and fight for genuinely affordable housing so residents can stay in the community and build secure, stable homes.

I have lived in Bexley Borough for over 40 years since leaving the Army. I have been involved in many community projects over the years, including experience as a Local Councillor, as a trustee of Crossness Engines Trust, Hall Place and Danson House. I also ran Bexley Village and Slade Green Community Libraries. I am now working on various river conservation projects across Bexley.

I will be a strong advocate on Bexley Council for the environment, for fairness and all members of our community.
Ahmad has a background in adult education and social policy in further education. He will focus on improving opportunities in education and supporting stronger pathways for local learners.
Katty is currently studying for a Master’s degree in Social Welfare. She will focus on strengthening support for families and improving local services in West Heath.

My wife and I have lived in Bexley for almost 20 years, and raised both our children here. In that time I’ve worked as a copywriter, a TV & film subtitler, a science fiction reviewer, a helpline counsellor, a website builder, and even run club nights across London.
Having twice stood for the Green Party before, I'm standing again because I want to improve the quality of life for everyone here in our little corner of the capital. 
Vote Green to make hope normal again!
